Also in the sixty-seventh Psalm: <q>The Father of the orphans, and the Judge of the widows.</q>

sections.heading: 114. That one ought to make confession while he is in the flesh

paragraphs.text: In the fifth Psalm: But in the grave who will confess unto You? Also in the twenty-ninth Psalm: Shall the dust make confession to You? Also elsewhere that confession is to be made: I would rather have the repentance of the sinner than his death. Also in Jeremiah: Thus says the Lord, Shall not he that falls arise? Or shall not he that is turned away be converted?
paragraphs.html: In the fifth Psalm: <q>But in the grave who will confess unto You? </q>  Also in the twenty-ninth Psalm: <q>Shall the dust make confession to You? </q>  Also elsewhere that confession is to be made: <q>I would rather have the repentance of the sinner than his death.</q>  Also in Jeremiah: <q>Thus says the Lord, Shall not he that falls arise? Or shall not he that is turned away be converted? </q>

sections.heading: 115. That flattery is pernicious

paragraphs.text: In Isaiah: They who call you blessed, lead you into error, and trouble the paths of your feet.
paragraphs.html: In Isaiah: <q>They who call you blessed, lead you into error, and trouble the paths of your feet.</q>

sections.heading: 116. That God is more loved by him who has had many sins forgiven in baptism

paragraphs.text: In the Gospel according to Luke: To whom much is forgiven, he loves much; and to whom little is forgiven, the same loves little.
paragraphs.html: In the Gospel according to Luke: <q>To whom much is forgiven, he loves much; and to whom little is forgiven, the same loves little.</q>

sections.heading: 117. That there is a strong conflict to be waged against the devil, and that therefore we ought to stand bravely, that we may be able to conquer

paragraphs.text: In the Epistle of Paul to the Ephesians: Our wrestle is not against flesh and blood, but against the powers and princes of this world, and of this darkness; against the spiritual things of wickedness in the heavenly places. Because of this, put on the whole armour of God, that you may be able to resist in the most evil day; that when you have accomplished all, you may stand, having your loins girt in the truth of the Gospel, putting on the breastplate of righteousness, and having your feet shod with the preparation of the Gospel of peace; in all things taking the shield of faith, in which you may extinguish all the fiery darts of the most wicked one; and take the helmet of salvation, and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God.
paragraphs.html: In the Epistle of Paul to the Ephesians: <q>Our wrestle is not against flesh and blood, but against the powers and princes of this world, and of this darkness; against the spiritual things of wickedness in the heavenly places. sections.heading: 118. Also of Antichrist, that he will come as a man

paragraphs.text: In Isaiah: This is the man who arouses the earth, who disturbs kings, who makes the whole earth a desert. ]
paragraphs.html: In Isaiah: <q>This is the man who arouses the earth, who disturbs kings, who makes the whole earth a desert.</q> ]

sections.heading: 119. That the yoke of the law was heavy, which is cast off by us, and that the Lord's yoke is easy, which is taken up by us

paragraphs.text: In the second Psalm: Wherefore have the heathen been in tumult, and the peoples meditated vain things? The kings of the earth have stood up, and their princes have been gathered together against the Lord, and against His Christ. Let us break their bonds asunder, and cast away from us their yoke. Also in the Gospel according to Matthew: Come unto me, you who labour and are burdened, and I will make you to rest. Take my yoke upon you, and learn of me: for I am meek and lowly of heart, and you shall find rest for your souls. For my yoke is good, and my burden is light. Also in the Acts of the Apostles: It seemed good to the Holy Ghost, and to us, to impose upon you no other burden than those things which are of necessity, that you should abstain from idolatries, from shedding of blood, and from fornication. And whatsoever you would not to be done unto you, do not to others.

sections.heading: 120. That we are to be urgent in prayers

paragraphs.text: In the Epistle of Paul to the Colossians: Be instant in prayer, and watch therein. Also in the first Psalm: But in the law of the Lord is his will, and in His law will he meditate day and night.
